name: ACE Cash Express
description_short: ACE Cash Express San Antonio, Texas — ACE Cash Express is a check cashing and short-term lending retailer offering payday loans, installment loans, prep...
description_long:
Founded in 1968 and headquartered in Irving, Texas, ACE Cash Express is one of the largest alternative financial services retailers in the United States, operating under Populus Financial Group, Inc. With over 1,500 retail locations across 36 states and an online lending platform, ACE serves primarily unbanked and underbanked consumers who lack access to traditional banking services. The company holds state-issued licenses in every market it operates — mandatory for payday and title lending — and is specifically licensed under California's Department of Financial Protection and Innovation under Financial Code Section 23005(a). ACE also sells franchise opportunities, with total investment running approximately $143,000–$177,000.

ACE's core services include check cashing (including hard-to-cash checks), payday loans, installment loans, and title loans. Online payday loan amounts range from $100 to $1,500, while in-store loans run from $50 to $715 — with all rates governed by state-specific fee schedules rather than a single published national rate. The broader service menu includes money orders, bill payment, domestic and international money transfers, the ACE Elite Visa Prepaid debit card, direct deposit enrollment, tax preparation, prepaid phone services, and gold buying at select locations. Customers can apply online at apply.acecashexpress.com or walk into a store, and the ACE mobile app supports account management, mobile check load, bill pay, and ATM location.

ACE holds the distinction of being the largest check cashing franchise network in the US — a position built over more than five decades. Its most notable differentiator is its willingness to cash checks that banks and credit unions routinely decline, including government, payroll, insurance, and business checks. No credit check is required for most services, making ACE accessible to consumers with poor or nonexistent credit histories. The franchise model means local owner-operators run many individual locations, and annual revenue has reached approximately $695.9 million as of 2025, reflecting substantial scale.

Honestly assessed, ACE fills a real gap for consumers with limited banking options, but access comes at a steep price. Payday loan fees are regulated at the state level but consistently translate to triple-digit APRs — a structural feature of this loan category, not unique to ACE. In 2022, the CFPB filed a lawsuit against parent company Populus Financial Group alleging ACE hid free repayment plans from borrowers and made unauthorized withdrawals from customer accounts; ACE disputed the allegations and the matter remained unresolved as of 2026. The A+ BBB rating applies to the Irving corporate entity — individual franchise locations carry separate profiles that may differ. ACE is best approached as a last resort for consumers with no banking alternative, not as a routine financial tool.
address: 828 SW Military Dr, San Antonio, TX 78221

pricing.guarantee_details: No money-back guarantee applicable. Fees vary by state and service type; posted on in-store menu boards and available as state-specific PDF fee schedules on the ACE website. No application fee for payday loans.

certifications: State-licensed payday and title lender in 36 states
CA DFPI licensed under Financial Code Section 23005(a)

pros: Founded in 1968 — over 55 years in operation as the largest check cashing franchise network in the US
1,500+ retail locations in 36 states plus a full online lending platform at apply.acecashexpress.com
Cashes checks other institutions routinely decline, including government, payroll, insurance, and business checks
No credit check required for most services — accessible to consumers with poor or no credit history
A+ BBB rating at corporate headquarters in Irving, TX
Mobile app supports account management, mobile check load, bill pay, direct deposit enrollment, and ATM locator
No application fee for payday loans; online loans available up to $1,500 with in-store option starting at $50
cons: Payday loan fees translate to triple-digit APRs — among the most expensive borrowing options available to consumers
2022 CFPB lawsuit against parent Populus Financial Group alleged hidden repayment plans and unauthorized account withdrawals; litigation status unresolved as of 2026
Loan pricing is not centrally published — fees vary by state and require checking local store boards or downloading state-specific PDFs
In-store loan amounts capped at $715, limiting usefulness for larger emergency expenses
Franchise model means service quality and hours can vary significantly by individual location
best_for: Unbanked or underbanked consumers who need to cash checks without a traditional bank account
Consumers needing immediate short-term cash ($100–$1,500) who cannot qualify for conventional bank loans or credit union products
People who need to cash non-standard checks that banks or retailers typically decline
Consumers needing in-person access to money orders, bill payment, money transfers, or tax preparation services
diagnosis: ACE Cash Express is best suited for unbanked or underbanked consumers who need immediate check cashing or short-term cash access with no viable bank alternative. The core caveat is cost: payday loan fees are legally capped at the state level but remain extremely high, and the unresolved 2022 CFPB enforcement action against parent Populus Financial Group adds a material compliance concern. Consumers who can qualify for a credit union payday alternative loan (PAL), CDFI product, or personal loan should exhaust those options before turning to ACE.
services: Check cashing (payroll, government, insurance, business, and personal checks)
ACE Elite Visa Prepaid debit card
Tax preparation and tax check services
